codeReview.summary: The graph workspace and heavy visual layers were moved behind credible client-side dynamic imports with safe fallbacks, but the feature does not actually lazy-load the below-fold proof section it claims to defer. Because the proof section remains a Server Component, the implementation falls short of VAL-VISUAL-027's below-fold lazy-loading requirement and the new tests do not catch that semantic gap.

issues.description: `ProofModule` is wrapped in `next/dynamic`, but `src/components/ProofModule.tsx` is a Server Component (no `'use client'`). In the App Router, dynamically importing a Server Component does not lazy-load the Server Component itself, only any client children. Since `ProofModule` is server-only markup/data, the below-fold proof section is still not actually deferred, so the implementation does not credibly satisfy the feature's 'Below-fold content is lazy-loaded' requirement.

The new proof-section regression test only regex-checks that `page.tsx` contains `dynamic(() => import(...ProofModule))`. It never verifies Next's runtime behavior, so it passes even when the dynamic import provides no actual lazy-loading for a Server Component. That leaves the main semantic risk in VAL-VISUAL-027 unprotected.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance encourages dynamic imports and lazy-loading for performance work, but the shared state does not record the App Router caveat that dynamically importing a Server Component does not lazy-load the server component itself. That omission likely contributed to the proof-section implementation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:41-43 and .factory/skills/frontend-experience-worker/SKILL.md:35 tell workers to use dynamic imports/lazy-load heavy components; Next docs at node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/02-guides/lazy-loading.md:76-77 state that only client children of a dynamically imported Server Component are lazy-loaded; src/app/page.tsx:21-25 applies `dynamic()` to src/components/ProofModule.tsx:1-16, which is server-only.

summary: Fail. Client-side splitting for the graph workspace and heavy visual layers looks sound, but the claimed below-fold `ProofModule` lazy loading is not real in Next App Router because the target is a Server Component. The accompanying test only checks source syntax, so the regression remains unguarded.
